deployment_question: How does the model perform on unseen drugs, unseen targets, or unseen drug-target pairs?
definition: Split interaction data by holding out all records for drugs, targets, or drug-target pairs, creating cold-start settings that test entity-level transfer.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: sets same-entity train overlap to zero along the chosen entity axis

implementation_recipe: Choose cold-drug, cold-target, cold-pair, or double-cold setting.
Assign all records containing held-out entities to the test split.
Measure residual drug and target similarity to training entities.
quality_gates: Report which entity axis is held out.
Check whether targets or drugs have close homologs/analogs in train.
Avoid mixing cold-start evaluation with random pair leakage.
failure_modes: Cold pair is easier than cold drug or cold target if both entities appear separately in training.
Protein family or chemical analog leakage can remain.
Sparse entities can create unstable estimates.

citations.title: Improving the Assessment of Deep Learning Models in Drug-Target Interaction Prediction
citations.evidence: Proposes separating test samples by drug and target to better assess OOD drug-target prediction.

citations.title: Rethinking the generalization of drug target affinity prediction via similarity aware evaluation
citations.evidence: Shows standard DTA evaluation can overstate performance and recommends similarity-aware evaluation.
